channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Makgopa secures late penalty as Orlando Pirates edge Richards Bay to regain top spot
item.description: Orlando Pirates secured a hard-fought 2-0 victory over Richards Bay FC at Orlando Stadium on Wednesday night, courtesy of an early goal by Oswin Appollis and a dramatic added-time penalty by Evidence ...

item.title: Barry Keoghan clarifies pronunciation of his surname amid fan debate
item.description: Irish actor Barry Keoghan addressed confusion over the pronunciation of his surname during an appearance on BBC Radio One's Breakfast Show. Host Greg James questioned him after listeners and friends s...

item.title: NTSB: Driver Distraction Preceded Fatal 2024 Ford BlueCruise Crashes
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) released documents on March 11, 2026, detailing two fatal crashes in 2024 involving Ford Mustang Mach-E vehicles using the BlueCruise hands-free driving...

item.title: Florida Woman Charged with Attempted Murder After Shooting at Rihanna's Los Angeles Home
item.description: A 35-year-old woman named Ivanna Lisette Ortiz from Orlando, Florida, has been formally charged following an incident where shots were fired at the Los Angeles home of pop superstar Rihanna on Sunday ...

item.title: Embakasi Family Receives Son's Body After Hospital Bill Dispute
item.description: A family from Embakasi East can finally lay their son Joel to rest after his body was held by a hospital for over two months due to unpaid medical bills amounting to KSh 3.7 million. Joel, who was bor...

item.title: Multinational Poll Shows Mixed Global Views on US Reliability, Strength, and Tech Leadership in Early 2026
item.description: A large-scale multinational poll conducted by Public First from February 6-9, 2026, surveyed over 10,000 adults across the United Kingdom, United States, France, Germany, and Canada to gauge perceptio...

item.title: How ISPs Enable Mass Surveillance in Spain
item.description: This article examines the role of Internet Service Providers (ISPs) in Spain as central actors in mass digital surveillance. By providing the routers and modems that connect households to the internet...

item.title: SA Economy Expands 1.1% in 2025 While Manufacturing Faces Ongoing Decline
item.description: South Africa's economy grew by 1.1% in 2025, an improvement from the 0.5% growth recorded in 2024, according to Statistics South Africa. The final quarter of the year saw growth of 0.4%, largely drive...

item.title: Toronto’s Snow Piles Pose Significant Environmental Risks
item.description: Toronto has recently experienced unprecedented snowfall, creating enormous snow piles across the city. These 'snow mountains', some reaching the height of ten-storey buildings, consist not only of sno...

item.title: Key Findings from the Release of Mandelson’s Ambassadorial Files
item.description: The first set of documents regarding Peter Mandelson’s brief tenure as UK ambassador to Washington has been released, totalling 147 pages out of a much larger collection. The files reveal that Mandels...

item.title: ASX futures fall as US shares weaken and oil prices rise following IEA release
item.description: Australian shares are set to open lower, with ASX 200 futures pointing down 62 points or 0.7 per cent to 8668. In the United States, share prices were mixed, with the Dow Jones broadly lower and the S...

item.title: Government sends notice to Telegram for hosting pirated content on thousands of channels
item.description: The Indian government has formally issued a notice to Telegram, the messaging app, concerning the circulation of pirated content across its platform. Authorities have identified over 3,100 channels on...

item.title: Philippines to experience strong monsoon winds and cooler temperatures starting tomorrow
item.description: The Philippines is set to experience the final surge of the northeast monsoon, locally called 'amihan,' starting tomorrow, according to PAGASA. Weather specialist Benison Estareja said that cooler tem...

item.title: SIMS Nigeria to Launch New Digital Centre in Ikeja with Special Discounts
item.description: SIMS Nigeria, wey be one of the leading smart electronics companies for Naija, don announce say dem go open their new SIMS Digital Centre for Allen Avenue, Ikeja on Friday, March 13, starting from 10:...

item.title: Iran military actions escalate tensions, threatening global oil supply
item.description: Iran has intensified military actions across the Middle East, striking merchant ships in the Gulf and targeting locations in Israel and other regional sites. The Iranian military has warned that oil p...

item.title: Budget Cuts in Federal Prisons Will Reduce Librarian and Employment Support Roles
item.description: Correctional Service Canada (CSC) plans to cut library technician and employment co-ordinator positions in federal penitentiaries as part of broader federal budget reductions aimed at saving over $132...

item.title: SADC Finance Committee Reviews Regional Budget and Plans for Development Fund
item.description: Senior finance officials from Southern Africa, including a delegation from Tanzania led by Rishade Bade, Commissioner of External Finance, met in Pretoria on March 9, 2026. The purpose of the meeting ...

item.title: SSS Enables Pensioners to Complete Annual Confirmation Online via Facial Verification
item.description: The Social Security System (SSS) has launched an online facial verification portal to make the Annual Confirmation of Pensioners (ACOP) process more convenient. This new system allows registered pensi...

item.title: Military Strikes Alone Cannot Ensure Political Stability in Iran, Lessons from Iraq
item.description: The article discusses the fundamental distinction between military success and political outcomes, using Iraq as a cautionary example for potential action against Iran. While the US achieved all milit...

item.title: UNSC Condemns Iran’s Gulf Attacks, Calls for Immediate Dialogue
item.description: On 12th March 2026, the UN Security Council (UNSC) adopted a resolution led by Bahrain condemning Iran's recent missile and drone strikes on Gulf countries, which targeted US bases and assets in retal...
